name: perplexity-vscode
description: Perplexity AI search, reasoning, research, and compute — MCP server, dashboard, and multi-IDE auto-config for VS Code.

Perplexity.defaultSearchModel.description: Default model used by the embedded MCP server for flexible ask/search tools.

Perplexity.reasonModel.description: Default model for the perplexity_reason tool (multi-step reasoning).

Perplexity.researchModel.description: Default model for the perplexity_research tool (deep research reports).

Perplexity.computeModel.description: Default model for the perplexity_compute tool (ASI / computer mode).

Perplexity.chromePath.description: Optional absolute path to Google Chrome. When empty the extension falls back to auto-detection. Superseded by Perplexity.browserChoice for new installs.

Perplexity.browserChoice.description: Selected browser runtime. Managed by the dashboard Browser Settings picker; edit directly at your own risk.

Perplexity.debugMode.description: Enable verbose debug logging in the Perplexity MCP output channel.

Perplexity.autoConfigureCursor.description: Offer and generate Cursor MCP configuration when Cursor is detected.

Perplexity.autoConfigureWindsurf.description: Offer and generate Windsurf MCP configuration when Windsurf is detected.

Perplexity.autoConfigureWindsurfNext.description: Offer and generate Windsurf Next MCP configuration when Windsurf Next is detected.

Perplexity.autoConfigureClaudeDesktop.description: Offer and generate Claude Desktop configuration when Claude Desktop is detected.

Perplexity.autoConfigureClaudeCode.description: Offer and generate Claude Code (~/.claude.json) MCP configuration.

Perplexity.autoConfigureCline.description: Offer and generate Cline MCP configuration.

Perplexity.autoConfigureAmp.description: Offer and generate Amp (Sourcegraph) MCP configuration.

Perplexity.autoConfigureCodexCli.description: Offer and generate OpenAI Codex CLI MCP configuration.

Perplexity.autoRefreshIntervalHours.description: Hours between automatic session health checks. 0 to disable.

Perplexity.debugVerboseHttp.description: Include HTTP traffic from the MCP server in debug events.

Perplexity.oauthConsentCacheTtlHours.description: Hours a granted OAuth consent is remembered before re-prompting. 0 disables the cache (modal every time). Applies after the daemon is restarted.

Perplexity.mcpTransportByIde.description: Per-IDE MCP transport selection. Keys are IDE target names (e.g., 'cursor', 'claudeDesktop'); missing keys resolve to 'stdio-daemon-proxy'.

Perplexity.daemonPort.description: Pinned loopback port for the embedded daemon. 0 = ephemeral (OS-assigned, changes on restart). Set to a fixed port (1024-65535) to make http-loopback configs stable across daemon restarts.

Perplexity.syncFolderPatterns.description: Extra regex patterns used to detect cloud-sync folders when generating http-loopback configs. Matches against the target path or any ancestor directory. Well-known patterns (iCloud, OneDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive, Syncthing, pCloud) are always detected - this setting adds user-specific ones.

Perplexity.autoRegenerateStaleConfigs.description: When a configured IDE's mcp.json contains a stale URL or bearer (after a daemon restart or tunnel change), automatically regenerate it with current live values. When disabled, the dashboard shows a banner and the user must click 'Regenerate all' manually.

Perplexity.enableTunnels.description: Enable remote access via Cloudflare or ngrok tunnels. When disabled, the dashboard shows only loopback (127.0.0.1) configuration; tunnel-related UI is hidden and http-tunnel is not offered as a transport option. Turn this on to expose the MCP server over the internet (with OAuth + WAF).

scripts.build: npm run clean && npm run build -w @perplexity-user-mcp/shared && npm run build -w perplexity-user-mcp && npm run build:extension && npm run build:mcp && npm run build:webview && node ./scripts/emit-version.mjs && node ./scripts/prepare-webview.mjs
scripts.build:extension: tsup src/extension.ts --format cjs --platform node --target node20 --outDir dist --external vscode --external patchright --external patchright-core --external got-scraping --external got --external tough-cookie --external header-generator --external fingerprint-generator --external keytar --external gray-matter --external express --external @ngrok/ngrok --external helmet
scripts.build:mcp: tsup ../mcp-server/src/index.ts ../mcp-server/src/manual-login-runner.js ../mcp-server/src/login-runner.js ../mcp-server/src/impit-login-runner.js ../mcp-server/src/health-check.js --format esm --platform node --target node20 --outDir dist/mcp --external patchright --external patchright-core --external got-scraping --external got --external tough-cookie --external header-generator --external fingerprint-generator --external keytar --external gray-matter --external express --external @ngrok/ngrok --external helmet && node -e "require('fs').renameSync('dist/mcp/index.mjs','dist/mcp/server.mjs')" && node -e "require('fs').copyFileSync('../mcp-server/package.json','dist/mcp/package.json')"
scripts.watch: tsup src/extension.ts --format cjs --platform node --target node20 --outDir dist --external vscode --external patchright --external patchright-core --external got-scraping --external got --external tough-cookie --external header-generator --external fingerprint-generator --external keytar --external gray-matter --external express --external @ngrok/ngrok --external helmet --watch
scripts.package:vsix: npm run build && npm run prepare:package-deps && npx @vscode/vsce package --allow-missing-repository --no-dependencies
